| Endpoint response codes | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-04` | — | `internal/engine/http_endpoint_response_codes.go`<br>`internal/engine/http_endpoint_response_codes_test.go`<br>`internal/engine/http_endpoint_synthesis.go`<br>`internal/engine/response_shape_go.go` | #3818: goResponseCodes resolves the literal status-code set from the hertz (CloudWeGo) handler body (located via source_handler). hertz returns via c.JSON(consts.StatusCreated,x)/c.JSON(201,x) and c.SetStatusCode(consts.StatusNoContent); the SetStatusCode verb + consts.Status* constant family (which mirrors net/http code values) were added so the named-constant set resolves (numeric literals already resolved). Honest-partial: a dynamic status var (c.JSON(code,x)/c.SetStatusCode(code)) is skipped — no framework-default 200 fabricated. | Request sink dataflow | 🟢 `partial` | `2026-06-04` | 3740 | — | #3872 extend-go-dataflow-sniffer-idioms: PARTIAL. Real hertz handlers name the *app.RequestContext receiver `c`, so c.Query("k")/c.PostForm("k") already share the gin-shaped key-getter arm; this PR adds the hertz bind idiom c.BindAndValidate(&req) to dfGoBindRe, whose bound root lifts its member field at the sink. Value-asserting test TestGoDataFlow_HertzBindAndValidateToDBCreate asserts field=Email -> db.Create. Boundary kept: TestGoDataFlow_HertzCtxQueryNotRecognised pins that a divergent `ctx`-named receiver's ctx.Query still yields NO flow. |
